summary.telegram_footer: 9 items surfaced | 23/23 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated

items.name: OpenRA
items.summary: Classic RTS remake rebuilt for modern systems.
items.why_included: HN commenters praised the remake’s balance, features, and faithful feel.
items.evidence: Multiple comments called OpenRA awesome or amazing and highlighted how well it balances compared with Red Alert 2.

items.name: Journey
items.source_story_title: Turn your site into a place people can bump into each other
items.summary: Subtle multiplayer game cited as a model for gentle online presence.
items.why_included: The TownSquare thread used Journey as a positive example of low-friction interaction.
items.evidence: Commenters brought it up as a delightful example of subtle social engagement.

items.name: Strava
items.source_story_title: Turn your site into a place people can bump into each other
items.summary: Fitness social network cited as a real-world town square for active people.
items.why_included: Commenters said it already functions like the product being discussed.
items.evidence: The thread explicitly noted that Strava can easily function this way and does.

items.name: Odigo Messenger
items.source_story_title: Turn your site into a place people can bump into each other
items.summary: Defunct IM service remembered for showing who else was visiting the same site.
items.why_included: A nostalgic commenter highlighted its radar-style shared-presence feature.
items.evidence: The comment called it a favorite late-90s messenger and described the site-visitor radar.

items.name: antiX
items.source_story_title: Linux on Older Hardware: The Complete Revival Guide
items.summary: Lightweight Debian-based Linux aimed at old and constrained hardware.
items.why_included: A commenter called it the top pick for truly constrained machines.
items.evidence: The thread described antiX as running with about 256MB idle while still offering a full desktop.

items.name: dotfriedrice
items.source_story_title: Linux on Older Hardware: The Complete Revival Guide
items.summary: One-command Arch/niri desktop bootstrap repo for a polished Linux setup.
items.why_included: A commenter shared it as the setup behind a usable older-hardware desktop.
items.evidence: The comment linked dotfriedrice and described a full desktop stack running on 2014-era hardware.

items.name: Plex
items.source_story_title: The case for physical media ownership
items.summary: Media server and library platform used as a self-hosted replacement for brittle lockers.
items.why_included: Commenters described moving collections to Plex + NAS.
items.evidence: The physical-media thread cited Plex as the place they moved to after losing trust in licensed lockers.

items.name: Movies Anywhere
items.source_story_title: The case for physical media ownership
items.summary: Digital locker that syncs purchased films across connected retailers.
items.why_included: Commenters used it as the surviving ownership model in the thread.
items.evidence: The discussion contrasted Movies Anywhere with earlier locker systems as a way to keep bought movies accessible.

items.name: UltraViolet
items.source_story_title: The case for physical media ownership
items.summary: Defunct digital rights locker that stored movie and TV purchase receipts.
items.why_included: Commenters used it as the cautionary example in the ownership debate.
items.evidence: A commenter explained UltraViolet’s locker model and noted that the service eventually shut down.
